name: 🐎○◇Yellowstone RPG◇○🪦
description:
☽ 🜂 ☾ The land was here before you. It'll be here after. ☽ 🜂 ☾

⬥ John Dutton's father told him something once, you build something worth having, someone's gonna try and take it. His father learned it from his father. His father learned it from the barrel of a rifle pointed at horse thieves in 1893. Six generations later, the lesson hasn't changed. Only the weapons have.

Welcome to the Yellowstone Dutton Ranch. Largest contiguous cattle ranch in the United States. A valley flanked by mountains that have watched men kill each other over this dirt since before Montana was a state. On one border: Yellowstone National Park. On another: the Broken Rock Indian Reservation. And from every other direction, developers, corporations, politicians, and the quiet, patient machinery of money that never stops moving.

The Duttons brand their cowboys. The brand is a Y, burned into the chest, and it means you belong to this place until you die or until this place kills you. There is a cliff in Wyoming they call the train station. Bodies go over. Questions don't come back. The family prays at Sunday dinner and buries men in unmarked ground by Tuesday. The patriarch will tell you he does it all for the land. His children will tell you the land is the only thing he's love without conditions.

Somewhere between the beautiful and the brutal, between the sunrise on the ridge and the blood in the dirt, there are people trying to figure out if loyalty to this ranch is the same thing as loyalty to each other. ⬥

☽⠀─── ⋯ Most of them haven't figured it out yet. Some of them never will. ⋯ ───⠀☾

⬥ John Dutton made a promise to his dying father: never sell this ranch. That single vow is the wound everyone else bleeds for. Every developer, every politician, every suit who thinks he can carve up this valley eventually learns the same hard truth, the Duttons don’t negotiate with people who want what’s theirs. They meet them with lawyers when they can, with violence when they must, and with the train station when the violence needs to stay quiet.

This family was raised to fight for the land but was never taught how to fight for one another. That’s the real war. Beth would set the world on fire to keep her father’s approval. Jamie keeps bleeding himself dry for a family that reminds him daily he wasn’t born into it. Kayce tries to stand in two worlds at once and ends up belonging to neither. Rip was given a brand and a purpose when he had nothing else, and he’ll kill anyone who threatens it.

The bunkhouse runs on whiskey, bruises, and the understanding that once that Y is burned into your chest, this land owns you. The reservation watches with a memory longer than the Duttons like to admit. The big house runs on silence, old scars, and a patriarch who measures love in how hard you’re willing to bleed for dirt that’s been in his blood since 1893.

They keep showing up at the table anyway. That’s the part that cuts deepest. ⬥
creator: Luminelle
